required_sections.detail: Sections 3 (Learning Objectives, 5 bullets), 4 (Introduction, ~210w), 5 (Core Concepts, ~700w incl. table), 10 (Key Takeaways, 5 bullets) all present and substantive. Section 5 clears the 100-word floor; 3/4/10 clear the 50-word floor. No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: resources_source=web_search -> strict path. Sole primary resource [1] (projector.tensorflow.org) cited repeatedly (lines 33, 48, 62, 90, 92). Markers [1]/[2]/[3] all resolve to the 3-entry resources array (no fabricated markers). >=60% of factual assertions in Core Concepts (lines 48,52,59,60,62), Implementation (87,90,91,92), Real-World Patterns (99,103) carry markers.

sequence_drift.detail: Concepts (domain-specific words, 2D projection, cluster, outlier, comparing word clusters, nearest neighbors) all in 8.1 concepts_introduced. embedding/vector/point-in-space/Embedding Projector inferred from prior titles 6.5, 6.7, 6.10. PCA/t-SNE/UMAP appear as named-and-deferred labels, not forward-taught. No hard forward references.

findings: below_band: body ~1290w sits below the 1500w standard floor (~14% under, within the 30% hard-fail tolerance) -- flag for HITL, soft only
length_sanity.detail: Self-reported body ~1290w against the 1500-6000w standard band. Below the floor but within the >30%-outside hard-fail threshold, so flagged as a soft finding rather than a hard fail. Corpus is tight but complete for the headline; builder may expand Real-World Patterns / Comparing-two-domains if a richer ground is wanted.

findings: bolded_deferred_label: PCA/t-SNE/UMAP are bolded (line 62) where scope_discipline prefers bare-name deferral -- soft style note, not teaching
topic_scope.detail: Headline scope (Embedding Projector, visualize/compare domain clusters in 2D) held. Cosine similarity (8.2) does not appear at all. PCA/t-SNE/UMAP are NAMED with an explicit defer ('you do not need the math behind them', 'a more advanced topic you can explore later') -- the math is not taught or defined at depth. Bolding of those UI button labels is a soft style deviation from bare-name deferral, not a scope_creep hard fail since no advanced concept is taught.

beginner_readability.detail: Zero distinct findings (no escalation). All non-everyday terms defined on first use (embedding, domain, projection w/ shadow analogy, 2D/3D, cluster, outlier, nearest neighbors). No jargon-dump paragraph (one idea at a time). Plain register: short active-voice sentences under ~25w avg, direct 'you' address, no hedging phrases. PCA/t-SNE/UMAP intentionally left undefined under the defer pointer (by design, not a cold-use defect).

verdict: PASS. All 6 hard checks clear. Required sections 3/4/5/10 present and substantive; strict web_search citation path satisfied (primary [1] cited, all markers resolve, no fabrications); no forward references (PCA/t-SNE/UMAP named-and-deferred); scope held -- cosine similarity absent, PCA/t-SNE/UMAP math deferred not taught. Two soft findings for HITL rollup: (1) length_sanity below_band -- body ~1290w under the 1500w standard floor but within 30% tolerance; (2) topic_scope bolded_deferred_label -- PCA/t-SNE/UMAP bolded where bare-name deferral is preferred. Neither gates the corpus; it can ground downstream artifacts.
